hadiths.chapter: 30. THE CHAPTER ON THE VIRTUES OF PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "The Messenger of Allah {saws} used to say:  
"Whoever devotes himself to a prescribed prayer, waiting for its time, then prays it at 
its first appointed time, completes its bowing (ruku'), prostration (Sujood), and 
attentiveness (khushu'), and then glorifies, magnifies, and praises Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Majestic, until the time for the next prayer arrives without engaging in idle 
talk in between, Allah {SWT} will record for him the reward of a pilgrim (Hajj) and one who 
performs 'Umrah, and he will be among the people of 'Illiyyīn (the highest r anks in 
Paradise)." 
[AL SADUQ] 
These reports have been presented along with what I narrated regarding their meaning 
in the Chapter of the virtues of prayer. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 211 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 34 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 116

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 30, H. 642

hadiths.chapter: 31. THE REASON FOR OBLIGATION OF FIVE PRAYERS AT
hadiths.ar:
644  -  مَّا رَّوَّإةُّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال -  «
سَّوْذَّإءُّ ق ِى  وَّج ْهِهِ مِن ْ ق َّزْن ِهِ إ ِلَّى ق َّذَّمِهِ ق َّطَّالَّ جُّز ْن ُّهُّ وَّ ن ُّكَّاو ُّةُّ عَّلَّى مَّا ط َّهَّزَّ ن ِهِ ق َّا َّي َّاةُّ ج َّن ْزَّن ِي ل   عَّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ مَّا ي ُّن ْكِي ك َّ ي َّا 
إ ذَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ مِن ْ هَّذ ِةِ إَّلس  َّا مَّه ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ط َّهَّزَّب ِ ن ِى  ق َّالَّ ق ُّمْ ي َّا إ ذَّمُّ ق َّصَّلِّ ق َّهَّذ َّإ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلْأ ُّولَّى ق َّق َّامَّ ق َّصَّل َّى ق َّان ْحَّط َّث ِ  
إَّلس  َّامَّه ُّ إ ِلَّى عُّن ُّق ِهِ ق َّح َّاءَّةُّ ق ِ ى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلي  َّان ِي َّه ِ ق َّق َّالَّ ق ُّمْ ق َّصَّلِّ ي َّا إ ذَّمُّ ق َّهَّذ َّإ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلي  َّان ِي َّه ِ ق َّق َّامَّ ق َّصَّل َّى ق َّان ْحَّط َّ ث ِ  
إَّلس  َّامَّه ُّ إ ِلَّى سُّز َّي ِهِ ق َّح َّاءَّةُّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلي  َّالِي َّه ِ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّا إ ذَّمُّ ق ُّمْ ق َّصَّلِّ ق َّهَّذ َّإ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ   إَّلي  َّالِي َّه ِ ق َّق َّامَّ ق َّصَّل َّى ق َّان ْحَّط َّث ِ  
إَّلس  َّامَّه ُّ إ ِلَّى رُّكْن َّي َّي ْهِ ق َّح َّاءَّةُّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلز َّإن ِعَّه ِ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّا إ ذَّمُّ ق ُّمْ ق َّصَّلِّ ق َّهَّذ َّإ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّ ة ِ إَّلز َّإن ِعَّه ِ ق َّق َّامَّ ق َّصَّل َّى 
ق َّان ْحَّط َّث ِ إَّلس  َّامَّه ُّ إ ِلَّى ق َّذَّمَّي ْهِ ق َّح َّاءَّةُّ  ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلْح َّامِسَّه ِ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّا إ ذَّمُّ ق ُّمْ ق َّصَّلِّ ق َّهَّذ َّإ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إَّلْح َّامِسَّه ِ ق َّق َّامَّ 
ق َّصَّل َّى ق َّح َّزَّح َّ مِن ْهَّا ق َّحَّمِذَّ إَّلل َّهَّ وَّ إ َّي ْن َّى عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّق َّالَّ ج َّن ْزَّن ِي لُّ عَّ ي َّا إ ذَّمُّ :  مَّي َّلُّ وُّلْذِك َّ ق ِى  هَّذ ِةِ إَّلص َّلَّوَّإب ِ كَّمَّي َّلِك َّ ق ِى  هَّذ ِةِ 
إَّلس  َّامَّه ِ مَّن ْ صَّل َّى مِن ْ وُّلْذِك َّ ق ِى  كُّلِّ ي َّوْمٍ وَّ لَّي ْلَّه ٍ ج َّمْسَّ صَّلَّوَّإب ٍُّ ج َّزَّح َّ مِن ْ ذ ُّن ُّون ِهِ كَّمَّا ج َّزَّج ْث َّ مِن ْ  هَّذ ِةِ إَّلس  َّامَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Imam Hasan ibn Ali {a.s} said that a group of Jews came to 
The Prophet {saws} and the most knowledgeable among them asked him about various 
matters. Among the questions was:  
'Tell me, why did Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, prescribe these five prayers at five 
specific times for your Ummah during the day and night?'"  
The Prophet, replied:  
"(As for the Dhuhr prayer), When the sun reaches its zenith, it enters a ring, and when 
it enters this ring, everything under the Throne praises the glory of my Lord {AZJ}, the 
Exalted. It is at this hour that my Lord {AZJ}, the Mighty and Majestic, sends blessings 
upon me, and therefore Allah {SWT} made this hour obligatory for me and my Ummah as 
prayer, as He said: 'Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the 
night' (Surah Al-Isra 17:78).  
This is also the hour when Hell will be brought forth on the Day of Resurrection. Thus, 
there is no believer who is in prostration, bowing, or standing at that hour except that 
Allah {SWT} will make his body forbidden from the Fire.  
"As for the Asr prayer, it is the hour when Adam {a.s} ate from the tree and was expelled 
from Paradise. Allah {SWT} commanded his progeny to perform this prayer until the Day 
of Judgment and chose it for my Ummah, making it one of the most beloved prayers to 
Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. He instructed me to give it special care among the 
prayers. 
"As for the Maghrib prayer, it is the hour when Allah {SWT} accepted Adam's {a.s} 
repentance. The time between when he ate from the tree and when Allah {SWT} accepted 
his repentance was three hundred years of worldly time, equivalent to a day in the 
Hereafter, 'A day is like a thousand years' (Surah Al Hajj 22:47).  
Adam prayed three rak'ahs: one for his sin, one for Hawwa's (Eve’s) sin, and one for his 
repentance. Allah {SWT} prescribed these three rak'ahs for my Ummah, and it is an hour 
when supplications are answered. My Lord {AZJ} has promised to accept the 
supplications of those who call upon Him at that hour, and it is the prayer my Lord {AZJ} 
commanded me to perform when He said: 'Glorify Allah {SWT} when you reach the 
evening and when you reach the morning' (Surah Ar-Rum 30:17). 
"As for the Isha prayer, the grave has darkness, and the Day of Resurrection has 
darkness. My Lord {AZJ} commanded me and my Ummah to perform this prayer to 
illuminate the grave and to provide light on the Sirat (the bridge over Hell). No foot that 
walks to the Isha prayer will have its body made forbidden for the Fire, and this prayer 
was chosen by Allah {SWT}, the Exalted and Majestic, for the messengers before me. 
"As for the Fajr prayer, when the sun rises, it rises between the horns of Satan. My Lord 
{AZJ} commanded me to perform this prayer before sunrise so that my Ummah would 
prostrate to Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, before the disbelievers prostrate to the 
sun. The hastening of this prayer is more beloved to Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, 
and it is the prayer that is witnessed by the angels of the night and the angels of the 
day." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 211 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 68 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 
14 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 7, p. 476 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 31, H. 643

hadiths.chapter: 31. THE REASON FOR OBLIGATION OF FIVE PRAYERS AT
hadiths.ar:
645 -  كَّي َّث َّ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِلَّى مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ ق ِي مَّا كَّي َّث َّ مِن ْ ج َّوَّإب ِ مَّسَّان ِلِهِ :   
«ن َّ ي َّذَّى ِ إَّلْح َّن  َّارِ ج َّل َّ ج َّلاَّلُّهُّ ن ِالذ  ُّلِّ و 
إَّلْمَّسْكَّن َّه ِ وَّ إَّلْح ُّص ُّوعِ وَّ إَّلأِعْي ِزَّإف ِ وَّ إَّلط َّلَّث ُّ لِلْا ِق َّالَّه ِ مِ ن ْ سَّالِف ِ إَّلذ  ُّن ُّوب ِ وَّ وَّص ْغُّ إَّلْوَّج ْهِ عَّلَّى إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ كُّل َّ ي َّوْمٍ إ ِعْط َّاماً  
لِل َّهِ ج َّل َّ ج َّلاَّلُّهُّ وَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون َّ ذ َّإكِزإً ع َّي ْزَّ ن َّاسٍ وَّ لأَّ ن َّطِزٍ وَّ ي َّكُّون ُّ ج َّاس ِعاً مُّي َّذ َّلِّلاً رَّإع ِن اً طَّالِن اً  لِلز ِّي َّاذَّة ِ ق ِى  إَّلذِّي ن ِ وَّ إَّلذ ُّن ْي َّا 
مَّغَّ مَّا ق ِي هِ مِن َّ إَّلْأ ِي ح َّاب ِ وَّ إَّلْمُّذَّإوَّمَّه ِ عَّلَّى ذ ِكْزِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ن ِالل َّي ْلِ وَّ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ لِن َّلا َّ  ي َّن ْسَّى إَّلْعَّن ْذُّ سَّي ِّذَّةُّ وَّ مُّذَّن ِّزَّةُّ وَّ 
ج َّالِق َّهُّ ق َّي َّن ْطَّزَّ وَّ ي َّطْع َّى وَّ ي َّكُّون َّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق ِى  ذ َّكَّزِةِ لِزَّن ِّهِ ج َّل َّ وَّ عَّ ز  َّ وَّ ق ِي َّامِهِ ن َّي ْن َّ ي َّذَّي ْهِ ر َّإج ِزإً لَّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّعَّاصِى  وَّ مَّان ِعاً لَّهُّ 
مِن ْ إ َّن ْوَّإعِ إَّلْق َّسَّاذِ .  
وق ذ إ ج زج ث  هذ ة إلعلل مسن ذة  ق ى  كي اب  علل إلس زإن غ وإلأ جكام وإلأ سن اب .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated by Al -Hussain ibn Abi al -Ala' from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said: "When Adam {a.s} was sent down from Paradise, a black mark 
appeared on his face from his head to his feet. His sorrow and weeping over what had 
befallen him continued for a long time.  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} came to him and said: 'O’ Adam, what makes you weep?'  
He said: 'This mark that has appeared on me.'  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} said: 'Stand up, O’ Adam, and pray, for this is the time of the first 
prayer.' Adam stood and prayed, and the mark receded to his neck.  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} came to him at the time of the second prayer and said: 'Stand and 
pray, O’ Adam, for this is the time of the second prayer.'  
Adam stood and prayed, and the mark receded to his navel.  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} came again for the third prayer and said: 'O’ Adam, stand and pray, 
for this is the time of the third prayer.'  
Adam stood and prayed, and the mark receded to his knees.  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} then came for the fourth prayer and said: 'O’ Adam, stand and pray, 
for this is the time of the fourth prayer.'  
Adam stood and prayed, and the mark receded to his feet.  
Archangel Jibril {a.s} finally came for the fifth prayer and said: 'O’ Adam, stand and pray, 
for this is the time of the fifth prayer.'  
Adam stood and prayed, and the mark disappeared.  
Adam praised and thanked Allah {SWT}, and Archangel Jibril {a.s} said:  
'O’ Adam, the example of your descendants in these prayers is like your example with 
this mark. Whoever among your descendants prays the five daily prayers, his sins will 
be removed just as this mark was removed from you.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 321 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 2, p. 338 • 
Jami’ al-Akhbar, Part 1, p. 66 • Al -Manaqib, Part 4, p. 265 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 71 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
15 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 11, p. 166 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 265 • Qisas al-Anbiya’ (li’l-Jaza’iri), Vol. 1, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 31, H. 644

hadiths.chapter: 31. THE REASON FOR OBLIGATION OF FIVE PRAYERS AT
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Musa al -Ridha wrote to Muhammad ibn Sinan in response to his 
questions, saying:  
"The reason for the prescription of prayer is that it is an acknowledgment of Allah’s {SWT} 
Lord {AZJ}ship, a rejection of idols, and a standing in the presence of the Mighty, the 
Glorious, with humility, poverty, submissiveness, confession, and a request for the 
forgiveness of past sins. It involves placing one's face on the ground daily as an act of 
magnifying and glorifying Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. 
The prayer ensures that one remains mindful, not forgetful or arrogant. It makes one 
humble, submissive, desirous, and seeking an increase in faith and worldly needs, in 
addition to making it obligatory and continuous remembrance of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty 
and Majestic, by day and night so that the servant does not forget his Lord {AZJ}, Master, 
and Creator, and thus does not become boastful or overstep limits. Standing in prayer 
serves as a deterrent against sins and a barrier from engaging in corruption."   
[AL SADUQ] 
These defects have been presented with evidence in the book of the Laws, Rulings, 
and Causes. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 214 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 72 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 8

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 31, H. 645
reference.chapter: THE REASON FOR OBLIGATION OF FIVE PRAYERS AT

hadiths.ar:
647  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ عُّن َّي ْذُّ ن ْن ُّ ر ُّرَّإرَّة :  عَّن ْ وَّق ْث ِ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ وَّ إَّلْعَّصْزِ ق َّق َّال 
إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن  َّ هَّذ ِةِ ق َّن ْلَّ هَّذ ِةِ ي ُّم َّ إ َّن ْث َّ ق ِى  وَّق ْث ٍ مِن ْهُّمَّا ج َّمِي عاً جَّي  َّى ي َّع ِي ث َّ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ .
hadiths.en:
Malik Al-Juhani asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the time 
for the Dhuhr (noon) prayer.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "When the sun declines (from its zenith), the time for both (Dhuhr 
and Asr) prayers begins. So, when you finish your Nawafil (supererogatory prayer), you 
may perform the Dhuhr prayer whenever you wish."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 646
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٤٦

hadiths.ar:
648  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ط  ُّهْزُّ وَّ إَّلْعَّصْزُّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ 
ع َّان َّث ِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ ذَّج َّلَّ إَّلْوَّق ْي َّان ِ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ُّ وَّ إَّلْعِس َّاءُّ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ُّ .
hadiths.en:
Ubaid ibn Zurarah asked Him, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, about 
the timing of the Dhuhr (noon) and Asr (afternoon) prayers.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "When the sun declines (from its zenith), the time for both Dhuhr and 
Asr begins together, except that this (Dhuhr) is before that (Asr). Then you remain 
within the time for both of them until the sun sets”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 19 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 
2, p. 24 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 246 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 260 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 68 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 241 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 126 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 647
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٤٧

hadiths.ar:
649  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّسَّارٍ وَّ ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّعْي َّن َّ وَّ ن ُّكَّي ْزُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّعْي َّن َّ وَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ وَّ ن ُّزَّي ْذ  ن ْن ُّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ إَّلْعِح ْلِى  ُّ  
عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ وَّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّمَّا ق َّالأ لط  ُّهْزِ ن َّعْذَّ إَّلز  َّوَّإلِ ق َّذَّمَّان ِ وَّ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلْعَّصْزِ ن َّعْذَّ ذ َّلِك َّ  
ق َّذَّمَّان ِ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"When the sun declines (from its zenith), the times for both Dhuhr (noon) and Asr 
(afternoon) prayers begin. When the sun sets, the times for both Maghrib (evening) and 
Isha (night) prayers begin." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 216 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 19 • Awaali al -
La’aali, Part 3, p. 69 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 242 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 125 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 183 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 648
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٤٨

hadiths.en:
Al-Fudail ibn Yasar, Zurarah ibn A'yan, Bukayr ibn A'yan, Muhammad ibn 
Muslim, and Burayd ibn Muawiyah al -Ijli narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s} and Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said:  
"The time for Dhuhr (noon) prayer is after the sun's decline (from its zenith) by two feet 
(shadows), and the time for Asr (afternoon) prayer is after that by two feet (shadows)”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 216 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 140 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 649
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٤٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The beginning of the time (for Dhuhr prayer) is at the sun's decline (from its zenith), 
and it is the initial time decreed by Allah {SWT}, and it is the best of them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 18 • Al -Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
246 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 207 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 120 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 46 • Mustadrak Al -
Wasail, Vol. 3, p. 104 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 650
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٠

hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "The beginning of the time (for prayer) brings the pleasure of 
Allah {SWT}, while the end of it brings the forgiveness of Allah {SWT}, and forgiveness is only 
for a sin."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 217 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, p. 195 • Awaali al-La’aali, 
Part 1, p. 413 • Awaali al -La’aali, Part 2, p. 213 • Meftah al -Falah, Part 1, p. 181 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 208 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 123 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 351 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 3, p. 100 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 651
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥١

hadiths.ar:
653  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْن َّاق ِزَّ ع :  عَّن ْ وَّق ْث ِ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ ق َّق َّال 
وَّق ْث ِ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ ق َّذ َّإك َّ إ َّرْن َّعَّه ُّ إ َّق ْذَّإمٍ مِن ْ ر َّوَّإلِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ  لِهِ كَّان َّ 
ق َّامَّه ً وَّ كَّان َّ إ ِذ َّإ مَّص َّى مِن ْهُّ ذ ِرَّإع  صَّل َّى إَّلط  ُّهْزَّ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ مَّص َّى مِن ْهُّ ذ ِرَّإعَّان ِ صَّل َّى إَّلْعَّصْز « 
إَّلذ ِّرَّإعُّ وَّ إَّلذ ِّرَّإعَّان ِ ن ْ ي َّي َّن َّق  َّلَّ مِن ْ ر َّوَّإلِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ْ ي َّمْص ِى َّ ذ ِرَّإع   
ق َّا ِذ َّإ ن َّلَّغ َّ ق َّي ْن ُّك َّ ذ ِرَّإعاً ن َّذَّإ ْب َّ ن ِالْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ وَّ ي َّزَّكْث َّ إَّلن  َّاق ِلَّه َّ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ ن َّلَّغ َّ ق َّي ْن ُّك َّ ذ ِرَّإعَّي ْن ِ ن َّذَّإ ْ ب َّ ن ِالْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ وَّ ي َّزَّكْث َّ إَّلن  َّاق ِلَّه َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "The merit of performing (prayer) at the earliest time compared 
to the latest time is better for a believer than their children and wealth."  
[REFERENCES] Qurb al-Isnad, Part 1, p. 43 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 217 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 40 • Makarem al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 301 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 207 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 12 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 652
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٢

hadiths.ar:
654  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّ لِأ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ :  «
ن َّق ِي  َّه   ق َّا ِن  َّ رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ ضَّ ق َّال إَّلْمَّوْي ُّورُّ إ َّهْلَّهُّ وَّ مَّا لَّهُّ ق َّالَّ  
« إ َّوْ ي َّع ِي ث َّ إَّلس  َّمْس .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah asked Imam Al-Baqir {a.s} about the timing of the Dhuhr (noon) prayer.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It is one arm's length (shadow) from the sun's decline, and the time 
for Asr (afternoon) is two arm's lengths from the time of Dhuhr, which is four feet from 
the sun's decline."  
He then added, "The wall of the Prophet’s Mosque {saws} was as high as a person's 
stature, and when a shadow reached one arm's length, the Dhuhr prayer would be 
performed, and when it reached two arm's lengths, the Asr prayer would be 
performed."  
He then asked: "Do you know why the measure of one and two arm's lengths was set?"  
I said: "Why was that made so?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "It is for the sake of the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers. You may 
perform the supererogatory prayers from the sun's decline until the shadow reaches 
one arm's length; when it reaches one arm's length, you begin the obligatory prayer 
and leave the supererogatory prayer. When it reaches two arm's lengths, you begin the 
obligatory prayer and leave the supererogatory prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 217 • Al-Wafi, V. 7, p. 227 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 4, p. 141 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 653
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٣

hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said to Abu Basir:  
"Whatever they may deceive you about, do not let them deceive you about the Asr 
prayer. Perform it while the sun is still white and clear, for the Messenger of Allah {saws} 
said: 'The one deprived of his family and wealth is the one who neglects the Asr prayer.'  
He was asked: 'What does it mean to be deprived of one's family and wealth?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'It means that he will have no family or wealth in Paradise.'  
He was asked: 'What does it mean to neglect it?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'By Allah {SWT}, it means leaving it until the sun turns yellow or sets.'"  
[REFERENCES] Al Mahasin, Part 1, p. 83 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 218 • Ma’ani al-Akhbar, Part 
1, p. 171 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 153 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 29 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 654
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٤

hadiths.ar:
656   -  وَّق َّال سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مِهْزَّإن َّ :  ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى  إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ إ ِن  َّا رُّن  َّمَّا صَّل َّي ْن َّا وَّ ن َّحْن ُّ ن َّح َّاف ُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون َّ  
إَّلس  َّمْسُّ ج َّلْف َّ إَّلْح َّن َّلِ إ َّوْ ق َّذْ سَّي َّزَّن َّا مِن ْهَّا إَّلْح َّن َّلُّ ق َّق َّالَّ لِى .  
 ووق ث  إلمع زب  لمن  كان  ق ى  طلث  إلمن ز ل ق ى  سق ز إ لى رن غ إللي ل ، وإلمق ي ض  من  عزق اب  إ لى ج مغ كذ لك  .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The time for Maghrib (evening prayer) begins when the disk of the sun disappears."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 218 • Al-Wafi, V. 7, p. 257 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 4, p. 179 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 655
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٥

hadiths.ar:
657  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ن َّكْزُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ع :  إ َّن  َّهُّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ سَّان ِل  عَّن ْ وَّق ْث ِ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ ق َّق َّال وي َّعَّالَّى 
ي َّق ُّولُّ ق ِى  كِي َّان ِهِ لِأ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ج َّن  َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلل َّي ْلُّ رَّإ ىٰ كَّوْكَّن اً ق ٰالَّ هٰذ ٰإ رَّن ِّى   ق َّهَّذ َّإ إ َّو َّلُّ إَّلْوَّق ْث ِ وَّ إ ج ِزُّ 
ذ َّلِك َّ ع َّي ْن ُّون َّه ُّ إَّلس  َّق َّق ِ ق َّا َّو َّلُّ وَّق ْث ِ إَّلْعِس َّاءِ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ِ ذ َّهَّاب ُّ إَّلْحُّمْزَّة ِ وَّ إ ج ِزُّ وَّق ْي ِهَّا إ ِلَّى ع َّسَّق ِ إَّ لل َّي ْلِ ي َّعْن ِى  ن ِصْف َّ إَّلل َّي ْلِ .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran said: "I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about performing the 
Maghrib prayer, saying, 'Sometimes we pray, fearing that the sun might still be behind 
the mountain or that the mountain might be covering it from our view.'  
Imam {a.s} said to me, 'You are not required to climb the mountain.'  
[AL SADUQ] 
It has been narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} having said: ‘The 
time for Maghrib is extended for those traveling to reach their lodging or who are 
leaving Arafat to Muzdalifah up to a quarter of the night”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 218 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 80 • Tahdhib Al-
Ahkam, Part 2, p. 29 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 264 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 266 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 259 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 198 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 57 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 656
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٦

hadiths.ar:
658 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ :   « .   
وكا ن  إلي لث  هو إلأ وسظ ، وإلن صف  هو إ ج ز إلوق ث
hadiths.en:
Bakar ibn Muhammad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
that a person asked him about the timing of Maghrib.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, mentions in His Book 
concerning Ibrahim {a.s}, 'When the night covered him, he saw a star and said: "This is 
my Lord {AZJ}."'  
This marks the beginning of the Maghrib time. The end of this time is the disappearance 
of twilight (Shafaq). The start of the Isha prayer is marked by the fading of the redness 
(twilight), and its end extends until the middle of the night”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 219 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 30 • Al-Istibsar, Part 
1, p. 264 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 272 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 174 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol. 2, p. 438 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 657
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٧

hadiths.ar:
659 - وَّ رُّوِى .    
وإ ن ما وج ث  ذ لك  علي ه لن ومه عن ها إ لى ن صف  إللي ل.
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Muawiya ibn Ammar it is stated:  
"The time for the last Isha prayer extends until one -third of the night”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
It seems that the one-third mark represents the middle point, while the half is the end 
of the time period. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 219 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 279 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 658
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٨

hadiths.ar:
660  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى إَّلْح َّي ْعَّمِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال -  «
وَّ إ لِهِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  إَّلْمَّع ْزِب َّ وَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  مَّعَّهُّ جَّى    مِن َّ إَّلْأ َّن ْصَّارِ ي ُّق َّالُّ لَّهُّمْ ن َّن ُّو سَّلِمَّه َّ مَّن َّار ِلُّهُّمْ عَّلَّى ن ِصْف ِ مِي لٍ ق َّي ُّصَّل ُّون َّ مَّعَّهُّ ي ُّم َّ 
ي َّن ْصَّزِق ُّون َّ إ ِلَّى مَّن َّار ِلِهِمْ وَّ هُّمْ  ي َّزَّوْن َّ مَّوَّإص ِغَّ سِهَّامِهِمْ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated:  
"For one who sleeps through the last Isha prayer until the middle of the night, it is 
obligatory for him to make it up (as Qadha) and he should begin the next day fasting as 
a form of penance”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
This obligation is imposed due to his sleeping through the prayer until midnight.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 659
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٥٩

hadiths.ar:
661  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ي ُّو َّج ِّزُّون َّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب َّ جَّي  َّى ي َّس ْي َّن ِك ُّ إَّلن  ُّح ُّومُّ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Yahya al-Khath'ami narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} used to perform the Maghrib prayer, and a group from the 
Ansar called Banu Salimah, whose homes were at a distance of half a mile, would pray 
with him. They would then return to their homes while they could still see the marks of 
their arrows”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 220 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 80 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 272 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 188 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 58 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 660
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٠

hadiths.ar:
662   -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو إ ُّسَّامَّه َّ ر َّي ْذ  إَّلس  َّح َّامُّ :  صَّعِذْب ُّ مَّز َّة ً ج َّن َّلَّ إ َّن ِى  ق ُّن َّي ْسٍ وَّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ ي ُّصَّل ُّون َّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب َّ ق َّزَّإ َّي ْث ُّ إَّلس  َّمْسَّ لَّمْ 
ي َّع ِث ْ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّوَّإرَّب ْ ج َّلْف َّ إَّلْح َّن َّلِ عَّن ِ إَّلن  َّاسِ ق َّلَّق ِي ث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّا َّج ْن َّزْي ُّهُّ  ن ِذ َّلِك َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لِى لِمَّ ق َّعَّلْث َّ  
ذ َّلِك َّ ن ِن ْسَّ مَّا صَّن َّعْث َّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي ُّصَّلِّي هَّا إ ِذ َّإ لَّمْ ي َّزَّهَّا ج َّلْف َّ إَّلْح َّن َّلِ ع َّان َّث ْ إ َّوْ ع َّارَّب ْ مَّا لَّمْ ي َّي َّح َّل َّلْ هَّا سَّحَّاب   إ َّوْ ط ُّلْمَّه   ي ُّط ِل ُّهَّا 
ق َّا ِن  َّمَّا عَّلَّي ْك َّ مَّس ْزِق ُّك َّ وَّ مَّع ْزِن ُّك َّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّى إَّلن  َّاسِ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْ حَّي ُّوإ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Cursed, cursed is the one who 
delays the Maghrib prayer seeking to gain a supposed additional merit."  
It was said to him that the people of Iraq delay the Maghrib prayer until the stars 
become visible, to which he responded,  
"This is among the acts of the enemy of Allah {SWT}, Abu al-Khattab”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 220 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 271 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 661
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦١
